plan:
- Quadcopter or Hexacopter carbon fiber frame with high-lift brushless motors, ESCs, and a flight controller running ArduPilot or PX4.
- Multi-spectral camera or specialized soil sensor payload including pH electrodes, dielectric moisture probes, and NPK optical sensors.
- Onboard companion computer like a Raspberry Pi or Jetson Nano for real-time data processing and GPS/RTK for precision mapping.
- Power system utilizing high-capacity LiPo batteries and a power distribution board with voltage regulation for sensors.
- Motorized linear actuator or servo-driven sampling mechanism to lower sensors for physical soil contact.
- Custom 3D-printed sensor housings, landing gear extensions, and a ruggedized vehicle-mount deployment cradle.

nodes.name: Flight Controller
nodes.description: Main autopilot running PX4 for autonomous flight control and sensor stabilization.

nodes.name: Companion Computer

specs.label: CPU Clock Speed
specs.value: Up to 6-core Arm Cortex-A78AE v8.2 64-bit CPU (1.5 GHz)

specs.label: GPU Architecture
specs.value: NVIDIA Ampere architecture GPU with 1024 CUDA Cores, 32 Tensor Cores

specs.label: Storage
specs.value: External NVMe SSD / SD Card (No onboard eMMC)

specs.label: Other Peripherals
specs.value: 3x I2C, 4x SPI, 1x CAN, 4x USB 3.2 Gen 2, 3x USB 2.0, 1x GbE

research.summary: The NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano is a compact, powerful System-on-Module (SOM) designed for edge AI applications, featuring an Arm Cortex CPU and an Ampere GPU. It provides robust processing capabilities for real-time tasks like multispectral data analysis and autonomous vision. Its comprehensive set of I/O interfaces enables flexible integration into complex robotic systems like the Soil Analysis Hexacopter.

examples.title: Getting Started with Jetson Orin Nano and Hello AI World
examples.description: This official NVIDIA guide walks through the initial setup of the Jetson Orin Nano Developer Kit, including flashing the OS, and then introduces the fundamental 'Hello AI World' project. It's a comprehensive starting point for running basic AI inference tasks on the device, such as image classification and object detection.

examples.title: Jetson Orin Nano: GPIO Blinking LED with Python
examples.description: A beginner-friendly tutorial demonstrating how to control the GPIO pins on the Jetson Orin Nano using Python. This project covers the basic concept of blinking an LED, analogous to the 'blink' example for traditional microcontrollers, showcasing peripheral usage.

examples.title: Real-time Object Detection with YOLOv8 on Jetson Orin Nano
examples.description: This tutorial shows how to deploy and run the YOLOv8 object detection model on the Jetson Orin Nano for real-time inference. It's a practical example of leveraging the Jetson's AI capabilities for computer vision tasks, crucial for applications like autonomous navigation or object recognition.

examples.title: Setting up CSI Camera and GStreamer on Jetson Orin Nano
examples.description: An essential guide for integrating and utilizing CSI cameras with the Jetson Orin Nano. This tutorial explains how to capture and stream video using GStreamer pipelines, which is fundamental for any project involving real-time vision processing and multispectral data acquisition.

examples.title: Jetson Orin Nano with ROS 2 and Lidar for Robotics
examples.description: This project demonstrates how to integrate the Jetson Orin Nano into a robotics platform using ROS 2 (Robot Operating System) and a LiDAR sensor. It's a more complete example showing how the Jetson can act as a companion computer for advanced navigation and environmental mapping, highly relevant for drone-based applications.

sourcing.note: Super Developer Kit, delivers up to 67 TOPS of AI performance.
Search result for NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano.

originalPrompt:
Autonomous soil analysis drone
Multi-sensor UAV mapping pH, moisture, nitrogen across smallholder plots. Deployable from vehicle

subSteps.title: Print companion computer and vehicle interface parts

detail.tip: Print the ASA landing cradles in a heated enclosure to prevent warping and ensure the corner guide geometry remains perfectly square for autonomous docking.
steps: Load PETG filament and slice the Jetson Mount Plate with 30% infill and 3 shells.
Orient the Jetson Mount Plate flat on the bed to ensure M2.5 mounting holes remain dimensionally accurate.
Switch to ASA filament for the four Vehicle Landing Cradle components to provide UV resistance.
Slice the Landing Cradles using 40% infill and 4 shells for maximum structural durability during docking.
Remove support material and clear out the 10mm damping holes on the Jetson Mount Plate.
Test-fit the M2.5 screws into the companion computer mounting holes to ensure a clean pass-through.
detail.summary: 3D print the Jetson mounting plate and landing cradles using specified PETG and ASA settings.

subSteps.title: Install heat-set inserts or nylon standoffs into Jetson mount
Assemble folding carbon fiber arms and center plates
Dry-fit multispectral camera to gimbal bracket
Apply threadlocker to motor mounting bolts
Solder main XT90-AS lead and ESC power wires to PDB
Route and crimp PWM signal leads from ESCs to Flight Controller
Construct high-current battery cable and verify polarity
Connect Jetson power input to 12V regulated rail
Wire CAN bus and UART telemetry links
Establish Ethernet link between Jetson and Camera
Flash ArduCopter firmware and configure hexacopter frame type
Calibrate ESC endpoints and verify motor rotation direction
Configure RTK GPS inject settings and verify fix status
Initialize Jetson Orin Nano and verify camera stream
Test MAVLink communication between FC and Companion Computer
Secure Flight Controller and PDB within center frame
Mount motors and props ensuring correct CW/CCW orientation
Install gimbal and multispectral camera assembly
Attach GPS mast and RTK module for maximum sky view
Mount vehicle landing cradle to vehicle roof rack
Perform final balance check and secure battery strap

projectDescription: This heavy-lift hexacopter utilizes a multispectral camera and RTK GPS to map soil pH, moisture, and nitrogen levels across smallholder plots. Controlled by an ArduPilot-based flight controller and an NVIDIA Jetson companion computer, the system autonomously deploys from a vehicle-mounted cradle for precision agriculture mapping.

tools: Soldering station with high-thermal capacity tip
Laptop with ArduPilot/PX4 Ground Control Station
assumptions: Builder has experience with high-current LiPo safety
Familiarity with UAV ground control software
Access to a flat, level surface for IMU/Compass calibration
Basic knowledge of Linux/Ubuntu for companion computer setup
